mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and stimulates corporate borrowing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when added to a baseline macro model.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day equity returns turns negative in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following inflation prints above 0.4%, or the negative return signal disappears when controlling for concurrent federal funds rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust labor market expansion, which supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises consistently coincide with negative 60-day equity returns, or the rolling correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real spending where spending outpaces GDP indicates accelerating consumer momentum, which precedes earnings upgrades and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spending-GDP divergence signal yields negative forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate output and profit expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for macroeconomic surprise indices.
